item.title: Amazon Seller Reveals Rare Glimpse of Shadow Bribery Market
item.description: Middlemen lurking on message apps like WeChat offer access to company employees they say can grant favors for a price.

item.title: Ramaphosa Taps Former Treasury Budget Chief as Economic Adviser
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa appointed former National Treasury budget chief Michael Sachs as his economic adviser, bringing a respected fiscal policy technocrat back into the government as the country seeks to accelerate growth and deepen economic reforms.

item.title: Microsoft Says Copilot AI Helped Knock Down Cybercrime Tools
item.description: Investigators used new tools to defeat old malware technology.

item.title: Bitcoin’s Retreating Retail Army Exposes Fresh Market Fragility
item.description: Bitcoin spent years trying to shed its reputation as a playground for speculative retail traders. Its latest selloff is exposing the tradeoff: Wall Street brought scale and legitimacy, but the retail buyers who once helped absorb sharp declines have largely vanished.

item.title: Mexico’s Annual Inflation Eases More Than Expected Ahead of Rate Decision
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation eased far more than expected in early June, according to a report that comes one day before the central bank is expected to hold its interest rate steady at the lowest level since 2022.

item.title: Bessent Says Iran Talks Feature Shift to Dollar Invoicing
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated that US talks with Iran include the longtime American adversary joining the dollar system and invoicing its oil sales in the greenback.

item.title: Qualcomm to Buy Modular For $3.9 Billion to Help AI Push
item.description: Qualcomm Inc. said it is acquiring Modular Inc., for about $3.9 billion in stock, adding artificial intelligence software capabilities that will aid its push into data centers.

item.title: Biotech Visionary Is Skeptical About AI’s Impact on Medical Innovation
item.description: The inventor of the groundbreaking Crispr gene-editing technology is skeptical about artificial intelligence replacing human effort in the realm of scientific discovery, adding to a growing debate over its impact on medical research and innovation.

item.title: Latin America’s Two Oil Giants Team Up in Search for Their Next Boost
item.description: Petrobras and Pemex have agreed to work together to discover, produce and refine oil.

item.title: Canada’s AtkinsRealis Seeks US Approval for Nuclear Tech to Power AI Boom
item.description: The surge in new data centers for artificial intelligence is translating into growth for the US electricity sector, and Canadian nuclear company AtkinsRealis Group Inc. wants in.

item.title: ‘Essex Boys’ Oil Traders Make Charity Offer to End UK Regulatory Probe
item.description: A group of British commodity day traders have offered to donate £1 million ($1.3 million) to charity to close a UK competition probe that looked at whether the sharing of potentially sensitive information distorted markets.

item.title: Dubai Holding Weighs Stake in Bain’s Data Center Group Hscale
item.description: Dubai Holding is exploring an investment in private equity-backed data center builder Hscale, people familiar with the matter said, as it seeks to grow its European portfolio.

item.title: Lingyi iTech Raises $1.1 Billion With Shares Priced at Maximum
item.description: China’s Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. raised HK$8.3 billion ($1.1 billion) after pricing its Hong Kong listing at the maximum price, with strong investor demand leading the company to reject more than 100 orders, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: EU Proposes Tax Simplification Measures in Bid to Boost Business
item.description: The European Union unveiled a new package to overhaul the bloc’s tax rules on Wednesday, the latest effort by Brussels to cut red tape and reduce the regulatory burden on business.

item.title: Fight Against Child Predators Gets Short Shrift While Cases Explode in AI Era
item.description: Budgets for investigators are falling behind as AI floods the internet with images

item.title: Waymo Charging Partner Terawatt Raises Up to $300 Million in Debt
item.description: Terawatt Infrastructure Inc., a company that provides chargers for electric cars and counts Waymo as a customer, said it is borrowing as much as $300 million from banks to support the acquisition and development of charging facilities in the US and abroad.

item.title: Heat Is on Central Bankers Setting Rates
item.description: In a sweltering week for Europe, central bankers are getting advice on how to handle climate change.

item.title: Maersk Looks to Widen its Foothold in Vietnam’s Container Ports
item.description: The Southeast Asian economy needs investment in his maritime shipping infrastructure to boost global trade

item.title: Meta, Microsoft Lead $850 Billion Boom in Data Center Leases
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. and Microsoft Corp. each committed tens of billions of dollars in additional data center leases in their most recent quarters, adding to the massive sums the industry is spending on artificial intelligence.

item.title: Carney Squeezes Germany and Korea as He Dangles Huge Submarine Deal
item.description: In late May, a South Korean attack submarine slipped into a harbor in Victoria, British Columbia — a floating sales pitch for one of the largest military contracts in Canadian history.

item.title: UK Banks Set to Extend Rally Against EU Peers, Berenberg Says
item.description: UK bank stocks are well placed to catch up to the loftier valuations of European peers in coming years as they benefit from above-par growth in profitability and stronger capital generation, according to Berenberg analysts.

item.title: The Debasement Trade Is Unraveling and Kevin Warsh Is One Big Reason
item.description: In hindsight, the beginning of the end for the debasement trade can be traced back to Jan. 30.

item.title: Investor Who Scored 900% Win in 2008 Crisis Has New Big Short Bet
item.description: Hedge fund manager Lee Robinson notched a 900% gain during the global financial crisis by turning a $20 million position into $200 million with timely bets against the US subprime mortgage sector.

item.title: AI-Powered Emails Are Influencing Politics on Hot-Button Issues
item.description: From California to North Carolina, officials weighing policy changes are being inundated with comments via platforms that tout AI capabilities.

item.title: Scammers Are Using AI to Create Fake Auto Loan Documents
item.description: It’s yet another headache for US auto lenders facing the highest delinquency rates in years

item.title: AI Billionaires Score Win in $29 Million New York Proxy Fight
item.description: Artificial intelligence billionaires including venture capitalist Marc Andreessen and OpenAI President Greg Brockman scored a key victory in a New York City congressional primary that became a $29 million proxy battle over who will control the future of AI.

item.title: Fintech Unicorn Is Said to Tap Citi and EFG for Cairo Listing
item.description: Fintech MNT-Halan is planning an initial public offering of its Egyptian business in Cairo as soon as this year, according to people familiar with the matter, the latest firm preparing a share sale in the Middle East’s most populous nation.

item.title: Europe Heat Wave Strains Power Supply, Sends Prices Soaring
item.description: Europe’s electricity system is under mounting pressure from an intense heat wave, with extreme swings in power prices as soaring temperatures drive up demand and curb output.

item.title: Investors Maxing Out AI Trade Feed Stock Market Volatility
item.description: Investors are maxing out the AI trade, setting the stock market up for a volatile summer.

item.title: Japan Unveils $2.3 Trillion Investment Plan for Next 14 Years
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi unveiled a long-term vision for Japan’s economic development featuring massive investment in artificial intelligence and semiconductors as well as other key sectors including defense, space and shipbuilding.
